behavior_summary: Handles the 'U8P_NITSTAND::look' (examine) event for a nightstand item: when an actor inspects the nightstand the script prepares context, marks the item state (set info) and excludes it from further processing (process exclude), then constructs the description prefix "nightstand " and invokes the item's bark routine to present that text to the viewer.

The handler passes viewer/process context (inferred from BP+06h), an item/object reference (pushed indirectly), the description string pointer, and the calling process id to Item::bark, captures the return value, performs an implies/pop sequence with the return value, then suspends/returns.

Transient resources (the constructed string) are freed after the call. The script's visible effect is producing the short description text for the nightstand; state-writing (info and process_exclude) appear intended to guard or annotate the item to avoid re-entrance/duplicate processing.
notable_control_flow: Entry on Event 0 (U8P_NITSTAND::look) yields a single linear sequence: set info -> process exclude -> prepare string -> push viewer+item+pid -> call Item::bark -> free string -> capture return -> implies/pop -> suspend/return.
The script captures and briefly uses Item::bark's return value (via an implies/pop) — its impact on further flow is limited to the immediate implies/pop sequence before suspension.
There are no observed loops or asynchronous spawns inside this handler; it acts as a short-lived process that performs state writes then delegates text display to Item::bark.
open_questions: Exact identity and numeric value of the 'info' key written by the 'set info' opcode (the disassembly shows a push of an info constant but its semantic meaning is inferred).
Precise object pointer targeted by the indirect push prior to set info/process exclude (which instance is being annotated: the nightstand object itself or a related object?).
Exact calling convention and parameter order of Item::bark in this engine (which pushed args map to pid, viewer, message, and object reference is inferred but not canonical).
Whether the process_exclude write is persistent across saves or only a transient in-memory guard, and whether other scripts check that flag elsewhere.
The functional meaning of the implies/pop sequence and the bark return value in higher-level game logic (does the return indicate success, text routed, or a branching decision?).

main_function.name: U8P_NITSTAND::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to the player/actor examining the nightstand by preparing item state and delegating a short description text (prefix "nightstand ") to the engine's Item::bark routine so the viewer receives the description.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_NITSTAND::look) — invoked when an actor/player inspects or 'looks at' the nightstand

function_map.name: U8P_NITSTAND::look
function_map.role: Event handler that produces the nightstand's examination text and marks item state to avoid reprocessing.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine called to emit/display the provided short description string to a target process/player; exact signature is inferred from pushes.

external_calls.purpose: Deliver/display the nightstand's examination text (prefix 'nightstand ' plus any item-specific content) to the viewer/process. Called with the constructed string, viewer/process context, an object pointer, and the calling pid (all argument roles inferred).

writes.name: info (set by 'set info' opcode)
writes.likely_effect: Annotates the target object with an info/state key (appears to mark inspected state or attach metadata to the nightstand).

writes.name: process_exclude (set by 'process exclude' opcode)
writes.likely_effect: Prevents recursive or concurrent processing of the annotated object (a guard to avoid re-entrance while barking/handling).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Handles the 'U8P_NITSTAND::look' (examine) event for a nightstand item: when an actor inspects the nightstand the script prepares context, marks the item state (set info) and excludes it from further processing (process exclude), then constructs the description prefix "nightstand " and invokes the item's bark routine to present that text to the viewer.
